title: Utterly Malty Middle-Eastern Goose Fat 2 Chickpeas Fried-Ball
description: A crumbly, meal-prep friendly falafel dish featuring the richness of goose fat and the earthiness of chickpeas. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a food processor, pulse the chickpeas until they are mostly smooth.
In a large bowl, combine the chickpea mixture, flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, and black pepper.
Gradually add the goose fat and water, stirring until a dough forms.
Cover the dough and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
When ready to cook, heat about 1 inch of goose fat in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Using a spoon, drop the dough into the hot fat, making sure not to overcrowd the skillet.
Cook the falafel for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Transfer the falafel to a baking sheet and bake for an additional 10-12 minutes, or until they are cooked through.
Serve the falafel hot, garnished with fresh parsley and a drizzle of malt syrup.
Serve warm or at room temperature, as desired.
To store, let the falafel cool completely and refrigerate or freeze for later use.
